title: Paths, Navigation, and Filesystem Hierarchy · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Locate application configuration and logs on a workstation whose current directory and distribution conventions differ from a familiar machine. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a path-resolution and filesystem-map notebook and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.
Use pwd, cd, and directory stacks while predicting the resulting working directory before each transition.
Relate root-level directories to boot, configuration, devices, variable data, user data, runtime state, and optional software roles.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — On a debug host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 0. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Resolve absolute, relative, home-relative, dot, and parent paths from a stated working directory.

Inspect the Absolute Relative Paths trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
